response:
The historic figure whom participated in surveying the borders of the District of Columbia and had a premier African American recreation center named after him is **Benjamin Banneker**. 

Benjamin Banneker was a free African American almanac author, surveyor, naturalist, and farmer who played a key role in the survey of the original District of Columbia. He was part of a group led by Major Andrew Ellicott that surveyed the borders of the federal capital district of the United States. His work in surveying and his contributions to the founding of the District of Columbia are commemorated in the **Banneker Recreation Center**, which is located in the Columbia Heights neighborhood of Washington, D.C.
